examples.title: Bubble Sort Algorithm
examples.description: Basic bubble sort with optimization using a swapped flag to detect if array is already sorted
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BubbleSort(A[0..n-1])
BEGIN
  FOR i FROM 0 TO n-2 DO
    swapped ← FALSE
    FOR j FROM 0 TO n-i-2 DO
      IF A[j] > A[j+1] THEN
        SWAP A[j], A[j+1]
        swapped ← TRUE
      END IF
    END FOR
    IF swapped = FALSE THEN
      BREAK
    END IF
  END FOR
END
examples.explanation: The outer loop runs n-1 times. The inner loop compares adjacent elements and swaps if out of order. The swapped flag allows early termination if no swaps occur, making best case O(n) for already sorted arrays.

examples.title: Selection Sort Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of selection sort showing minimum finding and swapping
examples.explanation: Selection Sort works by finding the minimum element in the unsorted portion, swapping it with the first unsorted element, and repeating. It always performs exactly n(n-1)/2 comparisons and n-1 swaps.

examples.title: Merge Sort Implementation
examples.description: C implementation of merge sort with separate merge function
examples.explanation: Merge Sort divides the array into halves recursively until single elements, then merges them back in sorted order. The merge function combines two sorted subarrays into one sorted array.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) in all cases

examples.title: Quick Sort Implementation
examples.description: Java implementation of quick sort with Lomuto partition scheme
examples.explanation: Quick Sort selects the last element as pivot, partitions so all smaller elements are on left and larger on right, then recursively sorts the partitions. The partition function returns the pivot's final position.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) average, O(n²) worst
